terms.definition: Var(ŷ): expected squared deviation of a model's prediction from the mean prediction across different training datasets; measures overfitting sensitivity.
Instability of ŷ(x) when the model is retrained on new samples from the same population; high values indicate overfitting to training noise.
Spread of a learned function or parameter estimate across repeated sampling of training data; the randomness is over datasets D, not a single draw of (x, y).
Low training error with high validation error driven largely by high Var(ŷ)—the model fits sample-specific noise.
Var(ε|x) in the decomposition: noise in y that no model can remove; distinct from reducible variance of ŷ.
E_D[ŷ(x)]: the average prediction at x over many training sets; bias compares this to E[y|x].
